blocks.code:
$ npm run release patch // without "--run" it will run in "dry run" mode
$ npm run release patch -- --run
$ npm run release minor -- --run
$ npm run release major -- --run
$ npm run release minor -- --preid beta --run  Use both bump and preid for first prerelease
$ npm run release -- --preid beta --run        For follow on prereleases of the next version just use this
export PATH="./node_modules/.bin:$PATH"
blocks.context: Or if you have this line

blocks.code:
$ release patch // without "--run" it will run in "dry run" mode
$ release patch --run
$ release minor --preid beta --run
$ release --preid beta --run
blocks.context: in your shell config, then you can run it just as:

blocks.code:
$ npm run release -- --only-docs
$ npm run release major
$ npm run release minor -- --preid beta
// or
$ release --only-docs
$ release major
$ release minor --preid beta
blocks.context: - to ensure there are no side issues before you release anything.

blocks.context: well as in the npm package.

blocks.code:
/* The following line can be included in a src/App.scss */
@import '~bootstrap/scss/bootstrap';
blocks.context: the bundler of your choice to compile Sass/SCSS stylesheets to CSS.

blocks.code:
/* The following line can be included in your src/index.js or App.js file */
import './App.scss';

blocks.context: you can create a custom Sass file:

blocks.code:
/* The following line can be included in a src/App.scss */
@import 'custom';
blocks.context: ... And import it on the main Sass file.

blocks.code:
<ThemeProvider dir="rtl">
  <App />
</ThemeProvider>
